output_text: A 7-year-old British schoolgirl, India Mayhew, has died after being thrown from a horse while on holiday in India with her family. The incident occurred at a riding facility in Matheran, 20 miles east of Mumbai, when the horse suddenly bolted and dragged her a distance, causing serious head injuries. Her parents, who were also on the holiday, chose not to press charges against the operator, stating it was an accident. The police have registered a case of accidental death, and the British Deputy High Commission in Mumbai is providing consular assistance to the family.

Austrian designers Maya Pindeus and Johanna Pichlbauer have created a robot called the "Beautification machine" that applies makeup, specifically eyeliner and lipstick, to a person's face. The device consists of three components: a lipstick wheel, an eyeliner applicator, and a mask that illuminates when the process is complete. While the machine is intended to simplify the makeup application process, it appears to have some precision issues, as seen in a video of women testing the device. The machine's creators showcased their invention at the Biennale Internationale Design Saint-Etienne 2015 in France as part of their art project.

A former US Army enlistee, John T. Booker Jr., was arrested and charged with attempting to detonate a car bomb at Fort Riley military base in Kansas. Booker, who also used the alias Mohammed Abdullah Hassan, had previously posted on Facebook about the "adrenaline rush" of dying in jihad and had expressed plans to commit an insider attack against American soldiers. The "bomb" was actually a non-explosive device created by confidential informants, and no one was in danger. Booker had enlisted in the Army last year but was terminated after the FBI questioned him about his Facebook posts and he waived his Miranda rights, revealing his plans to commit an attack.

England's push for victory in the first Test against the West Indies was led by Gary Ballance, who scored his fourth Test century in just nine matches. However, the spotlight shifted to Chris Jordan, who made a stunning catch to dismiss Darren Bravo, leaving West Indies at 98 for two and 340 runs short of their target. England's Alastair Cook set a challenging target of 438 for the West Indies, but they began their chase solidly, reaching 90 for one before Jordan's catch. England remains firm favorites to secure a valuable victory, but Jimmy Anderson's wait for a wicket to equal Sir Ian Botham's record continues.

Massachusetts Senator Elizabeth Warren expressed relief that Dzhokhar Tsarnaev has been found guilty of the Boston bombing, but does not support the death penalty. Warren believes Tsarnaev should spend his life in jail without the possibility of parole, stating that he should "die in prison." This stance aligns with her long-held anti-death penalty views. The case will now move to the penalty stage of the trial, where the jury will decide whether to sentence Tsarnaev to life in prison or the death penalty.

Tokyo's Narita Airport has installed brightly-colored running tracks in its new Terminal 3 to celebrate the 2020 Olympics. The cushioned tracks, which include directional information, serve as a useful navigation tool for passengers, with blue lanes leading to departures and red lanes to arrivals. The airport opted for this unique feature to save money, instead of installing traditional moving walkways or illuminated signs. The new terminal, primarily used for low-cost carrier departures, aims to provide an exciting and user-friendly experience for travelers.

Hull City manager Steve Bruce is interested in signing Leeds United midfielder Alex Mowatt, who has impressed this season with nine goals. Mowatt, a 20-year-old England U20 international, has drawn attention from several Premier League clubs and was scouted by Hull during a recent match against Wolves. Bruce is keen to bolster his squad with homegrown talent, and Mowatt is one of several promising Leeds players who could be targeted in the summer. Mowatt graduated from Leeds' academy in 2013 and has since become a key player for the first team.

Parma, a Serie A club, has been deducted four points by the Italian Football Federation (FIGC) for breaching financial regulations. This adds to the three points they were previously penalized, leaving them with a total of 12 points from 30 games. The club is currently at the bottom of Serie A and has been struggling financially, with players not being paid all season. Parma was also declared bankrupt last month.

Emily Grimson, a 25-year-old model from Shropshire, has built a lucrative career as a hand model, earning up to £100 an hour. Despite being one of Britain's most successful models, her face is rarely seen, as her hands are in high demand for product videos, TV advertisements, and magazine ads. To maintain her perfect hands, Emily moisturizes them up to ten times a day and follows a strict beauty routine. She has been modeling full-time since autumn and is signed with BMA Models.

Northern Ireland's tourism board is responding to comments made by actor Kit Harington, who joked that Belfast is "wonderful for two or three days" during an appearance on a US late-night chat show. Harington, who plays Jon Snow in Game of Thrones, has spent five years filming in Belfast, which is a principal location for the show. Despite his lighthearted jabs, Belfast has become a popular city break destination in the UK, thanks in part to the show's popularity. Tourism bosses have leveraged the show's success to attract visitors to the city.

Burnley manager Sean Dyche has encouraged striker Danny Ings to focus on enjoying his game, rather than putting too much pressure on himself to score. Ings has not found the net in seven appearances, but Dyche believes that if he starts to play with a smile, his goals will follow. Despite struggling for goals, Burnley has collected five points from a tough run of eight games, including victories over Manchester City and draws with Chelsea and Tottenham. Dyche is confident that his forwards will come good again and help the team avoid relegation.

Misty Machinshok, 33, has been sentenced to 15 to 30 years in prison for convincing her husband to repeatedly rape their 15-year-old daughter in order to conceive another child. Machinshok, who is no longer fertile, coached the pair on the best positions to conceive and even held her daughter's hand during the initial rapes. Her husband, Gary Machinshok, 29, has also pleaded no contest to raping the child and will be sentenced separately. Both victims are now in foster care, and the case has been described as one of the most heinous crimes a prosecutor has dealt with in 14 years.

Freddie Roach, trainer of Manny Pacquiao, believes Floyd Mayweather does not deserve to be considered the best fighter of their generation. Roach rates super-middleweight star Andre Ward and middleweight sensation Gennady Golovkin above Mayweather due to his history of "picking and choosing" opponents. Roach thinks that to be considered the best, a fighter must face the toughest competition, which Mayweather has not done. Roach is confident that Pacquiao will surprise Mayweather in their upcoming welterweight showdown.

Here's a summary of the article in 3-4 sentences:

Nathan Brown, a 19-year-old apprentice electrician, died after being electrocuted while testing lights at a factory in Rochdale, Greater Manchester. He fell 12 feet from a ladder onto a toilet block after apparently touching exposed electrical bars powering a crane, causing a fatal shock. Nathan was working with his father, an experienced electrician, at Eurofabs UK Ltd when the tragedy occurred in September 2013. He had been training to follow in his father's footsteps and was conducting a routine electrical inspection at the time of his death.

Scientists at Stanford University have created super-strong robots inspired by geckos, which can carry over 100 times their own weight. The robots, weighing as little as 9 grams, can haul heavy loads up vertical walls, with one model, μTug, able to drag a weight 2000 times heavier. The robots' "feet" are covered in tiny rubber spikes that mimic geckos' small hairs, allowing them to create a weak bond with the surface and climb up walls. These robots have potential applications in emergencies, factories, and construction sites.

Jarret Stoll, a 32-year-old hockey player for the L.A. Kings, was arrested on a drug-possession charge at a Las Vegas resort on Friday. He was charged with possession of cocaine and ecstasy and released on $5,000 bail. The Kings organization has launched an internal investigation and is withholding further comment until more information is gathered. Stoll has been with the Kings since 2008 and is reportedly in a relationship with TV personality Erin Andrews.

A man named Steve Spowart has been hailed as a hero after rescuing five horses from severe flooding in his rural town in New South Wales, Australia. The horses were stranded in deep water, and Spowart paddled out to them on his surfboard, braving submerged trees and bushes. With the help of the horses' owner, Sonia Sharrock, and others, Spowart was able to lead the horses to safety, despite facing challenges such as a startled brown horse that reared up and splashed him back into the water. The rescue efforts were captured in heartwarming photos and videos.

Four former Blackwater Worldwide security contractors were sentenced for their roles in a 2007 mass shooting in Baghdad that killed 17 people. Nicholas Slatten was sentenced to life in prison for his first-degree murder conviction, while Paul Slough, Evan Liberty, and Dustin Heard each received 30-year sentences. The contractors were accused of unleashing powerful sniper fire and machine guns on innocent civilians without provocation, with 14 of the deaths deemed unjustified by an FBI investigation. The convictions followed a lengthy trial with over 30 witnesses testifying against the contractors.

A 19-year-old man, identified as Moses Yitzchok Greenfeld, drowned in Hampstead Heath pond in north London after getting into difficulty while swimming with friends. Eyewitnesses claim that at least seven police officers stood on the bank and watched as the teenagers tried to save their friend, with some even diving into the water to search for him. The police officers reportedly did not intervene, and it is believed that the victim may have had a cardiac arrest due to the shock of jumping into the cold water. The body was eventually discovered five feet below the surface nearly six hours after he was reported in trouble.

Mary Day, a 60-year-old woman from Dorset, was found guilty of claiming £16,500 in income support and disability allowance despite having £27,000 in her bank account. She used the money to fund luxury holidays to Goa, India, and was caught after an anonymous tip-off to benefits officers. Day pleaded guilty to five counts of dishonestly failing to notify the Department for Work and Pensions of a change in circumstances, and was sentenced to 12 weeks in prison suspended for 12 months. She was also ordered to pay £165 in costs and a victim surcharge.

Two friends, Elvis Kwiatkowski and Chas Quye, have been sentenced to life in prison for the murder of Neill Buchel, a 39-year-old married father of two. Buchel was subjected to extreme and degrading pranks, inspired by the TV show "Jackass," before being killed and dismembered, with his body disposed of in a lake. The men had become obsessed with the pranks and had been making Buchel endure them for months before his death. Buchel had been living with a group of friends who were also involved in the pranks, and one of them, Scott "Gary" Hunt, was sentenced to five years for conspiracy to pervert the course of justice.

Apple has been granted a patent for a facial recognition system that would allow users to unlock their devices with a selfie. The system would use the front-facing camera to scan a user's face when the device is moved into a certain position, and automatically unlock the device if the image matches one on file. This method is similar to Android's "Trusted face" feature, but Apple's system would track the movement of the phone to determine whether it is being used or placed flat on a table. The patent was filed in 2011 and has now been awarded, suggesting that Apple may be considering implementing this feature in future devices.

Former US President George W. Bush visited a wounded soldier at Walter Reed hospital in Washington, D.C. during his presidency. According to his former press secretary Dana Perino, a grieving mother of a dying soldier screamed at Bush, asking why her child was in the hospital bed instead of his. Bush reportedly stood silently and took the mother's anger, allowing her to express her anguish before addressing the incident later on the helicopter ride back to the White House. Perino described the scene as Bush "expected and needed to hear the anguish" of the mother, showing his empathy and compassion.

David Marshall, a 68-year-old sex offender, has been sentenced to a minimum of 20 years and six months in prison for the murder of 20-year-old Eni Mevish, a forensic science student at Staffordshire University. Marshall, who was on licence for sexually assaulting a 14-year-old girl, stabbed Mevish in the heart, lungs, and liver after becoming jealous of her other friendships. The two had met six months prior to the incident while Mevish was out jogging, and Marshall had been providing her with financial support. Marshall attacked Mevish at her home after becoming angry when she was watching a film with a friend.

The Duchess of Cambridge is nearing her due date, and preparations are underway at her Norfolk home, Anmer Hall, for the new arrival. The nursery has been given a Beatrix Potter theme, with fittings from luxury children's furniture store Dragons of Knightsbridge. In a move that has sparked speculation over the baby's sex, Kate Middleton had several pots of pink paint delivered to Anmer Hall from Annie Sloan, including colors such as Henrietta, Antoinette, and Emile. The new baby will spend its first few weeks at Anmer Hall and also have a nursery at Kensington Palace.

Here's a summary of the article in 3-4 sentences:

The New York Police Department (NYPD) has revised its training materials, which are now being reviewed by a federal judge, to include directives against racial profiling, ethnic and sexist jokes, and stereotyping. The revised materials, a result of a 2013 federal ruling against the NYPD's "Stop, Question and Frisk" practice, aim to promote equal treatment and respect for all individuals. The training notes also instruct cadets to avoid imitating speech patterns of others and to use inclusive language. If approved, the revised training materials will be implemented for new cadets.

Crystal Palace manager Alan Pardew believes Manchester City can still win the Premier League title despite Chelsea being six points clear with a game in hand. Pardew points to past instances where City came from behind to win the title, including last season and in 2012 when Sergio Aguero scored a dramatic injury-time winner. He says the Premier League title race is never over until the end, and City's recent history proves that. Pardew's comments come ahead of Palace's match against City on Monday.

UK Prime Minister David Cameron responded to criticism from Church of England bishops over the morality of his austerity and welfare policies. In an Easter message, Cameron stated that the Coalition had spent five years trying to "lift people up" by making work pay and encouraging responsibility. This came after bishops published a guide criticizing the Coalition's welfare policies, including a £26,000-a-year cap on household benefits and tough sanctions for jobless benefits. The bishops argued that these policies unfairly stigmatize vulnerable individuals.

Former QPR manager Harry Redknapp has revealed that he resigned from the club in February due to more than just his knee problems, which he initially cited as the reason. Redknapp described the situation at QPR as "a bit of a soap opera" and claimed that there were people with their own agendas working against him. He also mentioned that constant speculation about his replacement, particularly Tim Sherwood, and the constant media attention wore him down. Redknapp believes he would have helped QPR avoid relegation if he had stayed.

Former footballer Dion Dublin made his presenting debut on the BBC's "Homes Under the Hammer" alongside regular hosts Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander. Dublin joined the show to help a brother and sister-in-law, Mike and Lucia, renovate a three-bedroom semi-detached house in Dartford that they purchased at auction for £215,000. Dublin seemed to be in his element, offering suggestions and ideas for the renovation, including overhauling the 80s-style kitchen and creating a kitchen diner. The show follows house buyers from the auction room to the completion of their renovation projects, with local estate agents valuing the properties at the end to determine if the buyers have made a profit.

In the Champions League quarter-final first leg, Juventus midfielder Andrea Pirlo provided a crucial 70-yard pass that led to a penalty for his team. The penalty was awarded after Monaco defender Ricardo Carvalho collided with Juventus forward Alvaro Morata, and Arturo Vidal scored from the spot to give Juventus the lead. The goal came in the 57th minute and was a result of a controversial moment, with initial contact between Carvalho and Morata appearing to be outside the penalty area. The win gave Juventus an advantage going into the second leg.

Manchester United manager Louis van Gaal has warned his players that Champions League qualification is not yet assured, despite holding an eight-point gap over Liverpool. Van Gaal sees next Sunday's derby against Manchester City as crucial in the battle to finish runners-up, and also as an opportunity to push for a top-two finish. He emphasized that complacency should not risk success, as United still have to play the other top-four teams. Van Gaal also hinted that Angel Di Maria may return to the starting lineup soon, but will wait for the right moment.
